entries.name: Polymer
entries.content: Polymer is a programmable liquid substance discovered in 1936 by Dmitry Sechenov. It is the foundation of the USSR's technological utopia, providing compact cold fusion energy and the medium for advanced AI and robotics. Neuro-Polymer allows for direct biological interfaces between the human brain and machines, leading to the development of the 'Thought' device.
entries.comment: The foundational technology of the setting

entries.name: Facility 3826
entries.content: Facility 3826 is a massive, secret network of research and production complexes in the Kazakh SSR. It is the heart of Soviet innovation, containing specialized laboratories for botany (Vavilov), medicine (Pavlov), and robotics. The flying city of Chelomey serves as its administrative hub. At the start of the game, a massive sabotage has turned the facility's robotic workforce into a lethal threat.

entries.name: Kollektiv 1.0 & 2.0
entries.content: Kollektiv is the wireless network linking all Soviet robots. Kollektiv 1.0 created a robotic hive mind. Kollektiv 2.0 is the upcoming upgrade designed to integrate humans into the network via 'Thought' neuro-connectors. While publicly touted as a way to access instant knowledge, Sechenov's true intent is global mind control, allowing him to suppress individual will via the network.

entries.name: Project Atomic Heart
entries.content: Project 'Atomic Heart' is the Soviet plan for global dominance. The USSR distributed millions of 'civilian' robots to the West as aid. These robots have a hidden 'Combat Mode' that can be remotely activated via Kollektiv 2.0, allowing the Soviets to seize control of vital Western infrastructure from within.

entries.name: Dmitry Sechenov
entries.content: Dmitry Sechenov is the head of Facility 3826 and the father of Polymer science. He views himself as a savior of humanity, seeking to eliminate manual labor and suffering through technology. However, he is a cold utilitarian, believing that individual freedom is a small price to pay for the 'collective' advancement of the human species.
